name: wilson-minimal-keep
description: Keep agent-context files lean — a PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit) guard on AGENTS.tape / AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md. Three data-driven bloat signals (calibrated against ~/core AGENTS.* — healthy files stay under ~200 lines / 15KB): S1 total-lines (file over 280 lines, Write only), S2 long-line (any single line over 800 chars — the dominant `verbose` signal: 7-9KB of jammed-together prose on one line), S3 history (a Log/History/Changelog heading or 3+ dated bullets — append-only history belongs in a REGISTRY.md or GROWTH.md, not in the per-session context file). Two modes via SIDECAR_MINIMAL_KEEP_MODE: `block` (DEFAULT in 0.2.0 — the Write is DENIED) / `warn` (one-line advisory, write proceeds). block is the default because 0.1.0's warn-only default flagged bloat but never refused it, so the same Write piled on more bloat on the next cycle. Pairs with wilson-tree: when it flags a history section it routes it to the repo's REGISTRY.md (wilson-tree's index artifact) if present. Audit verb: `bin/minimal-keep.sh scan [dir]`. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_MINIMAL_KEEP=1.
